programme.name: Social and Behavioral Sciences
programme.school: Tilburg School of Social and Behavioral Sciences

courses.name: Introduction to Sociology
courses.description: Core concepts in sociological theory, social structure, and inequality.

courses.name: Introduction to Psychology
courses.description: Foundations of cognitive, social, and developmental psychology.

courses.name: Research Methods I
courses.description: Introduction to empirical research design and qualitative methods.

courses.name: Statistics for Social Sciences
courses.description: Descriptive and inferential statistics using SPSS.

courses.name: Principles of Economics
courses.description: Micro and macroeconomic fundamentals for social scientists.

courses.name: Social Psychology
courses.description: Attitudes, persuasion, group dynamics, and intergroup relations.

courses.name: Cognitive Psychology
courses.description: Memory, attention, language, and problem-solving processes.

courses.name: Social Inequality and Stratification
courses.description: Class, gender, ethnicity, and power in contemporary societies.

courses.name: Research Methods II: Quantitative
courses.description: Regression analysis, survey design, and advanced SPSS/R techniques.

courses.name: Research Methods III: Qualitative
courses.description: Ethnography, interviewing, content analysis, and grounded theory.

courses.name: Introduction to Data Science
courses.description: Python for data analysis, visualization, and basic machine learning.

courses.name: Behavioral Economics
courses.description: Psychological influences on economic decision-making and policy.

courses.name: Organizations and Management
courses.description: Organizational theory, workplace behavior, and institutional dynamics.

courses.name: Health Psychology
courses.description: Psychological determinants of health behavior and clinical applications.

courses.name: Machine Learning for Social Scientists
courses.description: Supervised and unsupervised ML methods applied to social data.

courses.name: Public Economics and Policy
courses.description: Welfare economics, taxation, public goods, and policy evaluation.

courses.name: Philosophy of Science
courses.description: Epistemology, scientific method, and philosophy of social inquiry.

courses.name: Global Migration and Society
courses.description: Migration patterns, integration, identity, and European policy.

courses.name: Media, Technology and Society
courses.description: Digital media impact on social behavior, democracy, and culture.

courses.name: Ethics in AI and Data
courses.description: Ethical frameworks for data collection, algorithmic bias, and AI governance.

courses.name: Digital Methods in Social Research
courses.description: Web scraping, APIs, and digital trace data for social research.

courses.name: Neuroscience and Behavior
courses.description: Brain-behavior relationships, neuroimaging, and cognitive neuroscience.

courses.name: Political Sociology
courses.description: Social movements, political parties, voting behavior, and democracy.

courses.name: Advanced Statistical Modeling
courses.description: Multilevel modeling, SEM, and Bayesian approaches in R.

courses.name: Climate Change and Human Behavior
courses.description: Psychological and social dimensions of climate action and sustainability.

courses.name: Labor Economics
courses.description: Labor markets, wages, employment, and workplace inequality.

courses.name: Text Mining and NLP
courses.description: Natural language processing and text analysis for social research.

courses.name: Developmental Psychology
courses.description: Human development across the lifespan: cognition, emotion, and identity.

courses.name: Family and Intimate Relations
courses.description: Changing structures of family, partnership, and care across cultures.

courses.name: Internship
courses.description: Supervised professional placement in a research or policy organization.

recommendations.message: You're short on Research Methods credits. These are core to your degree.
Data Science skills are increasingly valued. Consider adding these.
Broaden your Psychology knowledge with these courses.
Sociology is core to this programme. Consider adding these.
Economics courses complement your Social Science profile well.
You're close to 50 ECTS! A few more courses will unlock milestone status.
